reviews.text:
Overall, I feel deeply disappointed with my experience. Throughout the entire process, I was constantly assigned to different doctors. There was no continuity of care and no single person taking clear responsibility for my case. Each time I had to re-explain my situation, which was emotionally exhausting.

Communication has also been slow and inefficient. Responses often take too long, and it feels unclear who is actually overseeing or coordinating the treatment plan. In a process that is already physically and emotionally demanding, this lack of structure adds significant stress.

The overall efficiency of the clinic has been low, and the outcomes have not met expectations. It leaves me feeling confused and helpless about how things are managed internally.

I understand that medical treatment does not guarantee success. However, patients deserve consistency, accountability, and timely communication — especially in such a sensitive and important journey.

At this stage, I feel more tired than supported.

My experience with King’s Fertility was very distressing, both clinically and administratively. On the clinical side, I did not feel there was adequate shared decision-making or clear explanation of options and rationale at key points, and parts of my treatment felt rushed. I left appointments unsure what had been decided and why, which is not acceptable in time-sensitive fertility care.

I felt pressured to proceed without a full discussion of alternatives and expected outcomes.
Medication/protocol decisions were communicated in a way that felt last-minute and not properly explained.
My concerns (including about timing/monitoring) weren’t taken seriously until I pushed repeatedly.

On the administrative side, communication was inconsistent and at times contradictory. I was told one plan by clinical staff and later told something different by reception/admin, including confusion around what was possible next and what pathway/funding applied. Monitoring and scheduling also felt poorly managed, and I repeatedly had to chase to get the right follow-up.

The worst moment was immediately after egg collection, while I was recovering from sedation and in pain, when booking/communication issues added unnecessary stress and felt lacking in compassion. Fertility treatment needs clarity, coordination, and empathy — unfortunately that wasn’t my experience here.

After two unsuccessful IVF cycles at King’s Fertility, one failed implantation and one early miscarriage, we decided to seek treatment elsewhere. Looking back, I truly regret the valuable time we lost before moving on.

At our new clinic, several issues that had been missed or not properly investigated at King’s were finally identified. Something as straightforward as checking anti-TPO antibodies had been overlooked, and once discovered, it was easily managed with levothyroxine. We were also concerned about my partner’s semen analysis. The sperm count of the sample that was used for egg fertilisation was suddenly 10 times higher than any of the past tests (including one just 2 weeks before). That was very concerning, nobody could explain it.

My stimulation cycle at King’s was difficult. I felt borderline overstimulated and, despite retrieving 21 oocytes, we ended up with only 4 embryos. At the time, I assumed this was normal. But at the new clinic, with 13 oocytes, we got 6 embryos. That difference made us realise things could have been handled more carefully.

What stood out most was the lack of transparency and explanation from the start. The whole process felt protocol-driven rather than personalised. Important tests and options, like sperm DNA fragmentation and PGT-A, were never mentioned until we researched them ourselves and asked elsewhere. I understand that doctors may doubt whether certain tests are medically necessary, but patients should still have the option to choose and pay for additional investigations if they might improve the chances of success.

Being an NHS-funded patient should not limit your ability to access or even learn about private options. Every patient deserves full information to make informed decisions.

A lot of people mention the lack of continuity of care at King’s. At the time, it didn’t seem like the biggest issue for us, but at our new clinic we saw the same doctor every time, and we now understand how valuable that continuity is. It shows that it is possible. The bigger issue at King’s was feeling like we had to research everything ourselves just to advocate for the right care.

I would have shared all of this directly with King’s, but they do not follow up with their patients. There was no chance to give feedback. This is especially noticeable because most clinics routinely send feedback requests and appointment reminders, which we never received from King’s. Allowing patients to share honest feedback is important if a clinic wants to improve and make sure future patients get the best possible experience.

Key points for anyone considering King’s Fertility
1.	Be proactive and educate yourself about your options, especially if you are an NHS patient.
2.	Advocate for yourself at every stage to make sure your treatment is personalised.
3.	Ask early about tests like sperm DNA fragmentation and PGT-A, not only after failures.
4.	Continuity of care matters, but transparency and proper investigation matter even more.

I hope this review helps other patients make informed decisions and encourages King’s Fertility to improve areas where it could really benefit future patients.

Overall experience: negative

Positive experience with Dr Kuga and Dr Sadaf: clear communication, evidence-based discussion, and appropriate expectation-setting.
In contrast, the consultation with Mr Kuhan Rajah gave a 5–10% IVF success estimate despite uncertainty about whether fibroids were cavity-distorting or actually blocking ovarian access; this felt premature without proper mapping first (e.g., saline sonohysterography, MRI, or diagnostic hysteroscopy).
Options and approximate probabilities were not presented (such as “Option A ≈ X %” and “Option B ≈ Y %”) to let us make an informed financial decision; instead, the discussion felt discouraging, as if there was no chance, with limited exploration of alternatives and statistics.
We would like to  transfer care to another clinic. The records-release process was described as potentially taking up to a month with no guaranteed expedition, which—given time-sensitive decisions—left us feeling trapped at King’s.
A close family member who is a retired gynaecologist agrees that prognosis should follow complete diagnostics and encouraged a second opinion.
Not recommend.

In response to kings

There no step by step plan. The body language and conversation was to discourage us to go further. So I’m not sure what you say to create a step by step plan. I understand physicians need to make recommendation but without the picture how can they make recommendations.

It’s been two months now since our first appointment. We don’t have any of our result that we requested. What’s the difficultly in giving people their result that they paid for!

I think the response from King's Fertility (Jo Evans) to my Wife's negative review perfectly represents how insensitive, unprofessional and inappropriate this Fertility Clinic is towards their patients.

There have been countless failures which are far too long to list within our agonising 6 months period with this Clinic. All of which, King's Fertility have taken no accountability or ownership for and have provided NO apology. Instead, deflecting it back to us and coercively trying to take advantage of our vulnerabilities by stating we are the problem. Ever since we raised a formal complaint addressing their failures and incompetence, on several occasions they've attempted to convince us to leave the Clinic which is no way to treat a Patient who's raised a complaint. They should want to acknowledge their failures and do better! I'd also like to know what the "additional support and appointments are" these don't exist, it's a false claim.  Instead they brush over and ignore statements which they know are true with regards to their negligence and poor conduct. They seem to think doing this disproves our claims, but it doesn't.  There is absent communication, lack of organisation, constant careless mistakes, staff are mostly rude and condescending/patronising. We've wasted 60 hours of annual leave, petrol costs not mentioning the £2.5k they kept on credit until we requested a refund. They state we weren't charged for cancellation fees (a cancellation due to their mistakes), this was only due to my Wife and I making a formal complaint refusing to pay, evidencing their negligence, otherwise they'd happily of taken our money knowing full well they shouldn't be. Furthermore, the appointment mentioned on 29th October was missed by my Wife and I as we were, again, wrongfully informed over the phone by Admin that our appointment was booked for 30th October. This Clinic provides no appointment letters, E-Mails or texts, which I imagine is due to the many booking mistakes they make on a daily basis, which they don't want the patients to evidence. When we have asked for booking confirmation we are told to "make our own note", I wonder why!  I had actually called on 8th November 2024 but again due to the Clinics incompetence no notes were made on the system to reflect this, Admin said this was being looked into as the calls were recorded, I provided the date and the time and I assume the clinic was found to be in the wrong because I’ve heard nothing more on the situation. We've requested our medical records, as you're never verbally informed of scan reports or blood results, if you're lucky you'll get a "all is OK", "it's normal". The Clinic also won't provide summary reports, scans or blood results after each appointment, which is standard process in nearly every NHS Clinic. The fact we paid privately you'd think they'd afford us with a piece of paper. Upon requesting our medical records we were told this will take 28 days as allegedly they have had a high volume of requests! I imagine it's due to patients either moving clinic or the fact patients are wanting to know their results due to not being informed in their appointments.

King's Fertility does have a dispensing service, but it's outsourced! Another false fabrication as a poor attempt of the Clinic trying to save their reputation. King's Fertility charge a £50 dispensing fee and won't allow you to take the prescription yourself to source the medication at the best price! You must only obtain all medication through their designated dispenser, the items are extortionately priced. Most Clinics will write the prescription and allow the Patient to rightfully have control of where they wish to obtain it whether it's via the Clinic's recommended supplier or ASDA!

I find King's Fertility deeply insult our intelligence and are very disrespectful to even attempt to try point the blame to us when we can heavily evidence our valid cause to complain. 2 months later, our complaint remains not upheld. We will escalate our complaint to the appropriate authority.

Appalling experience at Kings. I've left some time before I complete this review, as I went elsewhere for treatment after Kings and had so much better results. Kings are not good for women like me with low amh, put me on highest dose of stims to fry my eggs, made me trigger when I had three 16 mm follicles (they were apparently worried about ovulation) which resulted in no mature eggs and obviously no fertilisation, lost £9k with no results! And there was no empathy afterwards.  Went to another London clinic to embryo batch and each cycle I've had few mature eggs and good quality blasts to freeze, with them knowing to trigger me at LEAST 18mm follicles. Traumatised by my experience at Kings and would not recommend them at all!
My follow up consultation after this failed expensive round was the most discouraging, giving me a 10% success rate and saying it is likely my egg quality.

I can say that moving clinics was the best decision in my life as I have now been blessed with twins, using my own eggs! I would not recommend Kings at all.

I cannot stress enough how appalling my experience with King’s Fertility Clinic, London has been. I am beyond frustrated, and after filing a formal complaint that led to no changes, I am now considering seeking legal advice for potential misconduct.

In June 2024, I paid for an initial couple’s consultation with King’s Fertility Clinic, yet to this day, I have not received any actual fertility treatment. Instead, the clinic has endlessly pushed excessive diagnostic tests like bloodwork and scans, and even required me to get additional immunisations (boosters) and take iron supplements—both at extra costs. I've since learned these were unnecessary and had no relation to succeeding in a viable pregnancy. King's Fertility warned that they would refuse treatment if I didn’t comply with these recommendations (blackmailed).

King's Fertility Clinic has deliberately caused avoidable delays. For instance, not returning  my calls for weeks, set appointments with a 2-3 week wait, then claimed that my original blood tests were no longer valid under their policy requiring renewed blood tests every 4-6 weeks. This led to repeated blood tests at additional costs each time. Currently, King’s Fertility Clinic holds £2,500 of my money, yet none of this has gone towards the 3 cycles of IUI that I purchased.

When we finally attempted the first IUI cycle, I informed the consultant that I cannot ovulate without medication and need ovulation induction (e.g. Clomid). I provided detailed medical history from my previous NHS fertility treatments to confirm this. However, the consultant insisted on monitoring me to see if I could ovulate naturally. Throughout this first cycle, I had multiple follicle tracking scans, each showing immature follicles. Despite repeatedly asking/prompting about ovulation induction at each follicle tracking scan, I was dismissed by the nurse. At the last scan, the nurse informed me that my follicles were shrinking, and the IUI cycle would be cancelled. I was then charged a £500 cancellation fee. Solely due to King's Fertility incompetence, but I had no doubt in my mind, they did this deliberately to maximise profit.

Following a formal complaint, they switched my treatment plan to IVF. Yet, last month, King’s Fertility refused to begin treatment because I hadn’t had a period. They claimed I needed an “anytime” scan to determine the best time to start bleeding (an absurd statement) and insisted that I obtain progesterone tablets through them at an added cost. Despite having a prescription from my GP for Progesterone tablets to induce a bleed, they wouldn’t proceed with IVF since the medication was not issued by their clinic—another clear money-making tactic.

After I had a natural period, I contacted the clinic, only to be told by Admin that they need to have the consultant call me before starting IVF. That was two weeks ago, and I have yet to hear anything. It’s as though they’re frustrated that they couldn’t extract more money from me.

I wish I had taken previous reviews about this clinic more seriously. Five months of my life have been wasted, and I strongly urge others to avoid King’s Fertility Clinic. Their approach to exploiting vulnerable women desperate for a child to maximise their profits is utterly disgraceful. I'm absolutely furious and I'll be escalating my complaint.

I regret having to express my dissatisfaction with Kings Fertility. As a private patient, the client-facing and communication aspects of their service are notably very poor.

Despite assurances from the assessing Dr that a follow-up appointment would take 1-2 weeks at most - this was forgotten about and never offered; and resulted in having to consistently chase for it, then it was finally offered a month later.

Conflicting information from different receptionists regarding MDT meetings was frustrating, and challenging them resulted in them being insensitive, condescending and defensive. The telephone manner/etiquette of some of the receptionist is also very poor. The receptionists /admin staff will forever be the biggest downfall of the clinic - their attitude is very concerning and every interaction with them has been negative.

Furthermore, despite requests, no written correspondence or clinical letters from the assessments have been received, causing delays in necessary external referrals. The lack of continuity of care also makes it challenging to build rapport or trust with their doctors.

What is also concerning, is the absence of any formal ID checks.

This overall experience has been disappointing and has significantly impacted my confidence in their services.

Like many of the reviews our experience at the private clinic at Kings has been atrocious.  The staff are rude, unhelpful and provide inaccurate information.  My money was taken promptly at the start of the process but I have been unable to attain results, to decipher them and I am still awaiting a refund due to being advised to cancel one of the services I booked.  The result being that we now have no context of our test results or a refund.  I will be making a formal complaint and I sincerely hope that those who have to go through fertility treatment choose a clinic which is human, sympathetic and empathetic.  None of which King's fertility is.

Atrocious manners of receptionists at first floor.
My best friend was treated with urgent care after a routine appointment and was kept overnight.
I came to see her to bring her food and water; at first when explained I didn’t have an appointment they dismissed  me like a nuisance, then when they saw me seating and waiting for my friend to pick me up at reception, they proceeded to harass me, insinuating I was here uninvited and literally escorted me out saying they needed to limit the number of people on the floor (there was absolutely no one else but me and them 2!)
I attended this clinic 2 years ago when I had my son and even through the pandemic the service was exceptionally good,  these  2 lacked of compassion and professionalism and common sense. What a shame.

Absolutely rubbish service. Before making the payments they act really busy. Once payments gone thru they are available when ever needed. After the tranfer when we got a negative result they acted like they didn't have anything to do with …

As previous reviews warn - This place is just a bad clinic. If you have a choice you should avoid coming here. Horrible treatment - after waiting more than 30 mins for a blood test (which we paid for privately) 3 different ppl walked in and …
Doctors and Nurses are lovely but a clear written treatment plan was not provided. Felt very confused with each visit as each Doctor would say something different. …

Unpleasant results and follow up review day given was on phone instead of face to face. While the day given for face to face was too far, about 25 days from the day unpleasant results was announced on phone.
No reason was given for the cause of the ivf procedures failure. No authoritative advice on the way forward.
We were just put in the dark 🌑.

DO NOT GO TO THIS CLINIC ITS A SCAM!!!

I have booked an appointment through NHS and I have waiting for 4 months for …

Awful receptionist, she literally aggressed me when I asked questions about medical processes. Then a nurse came over and started speaking slowly as if my nationality (French) was impacting my understanding. …
Unsatisfied treatment plan, no investigation or explanation as to why treatment failed. No after care and a sudden stop with interaction. Unprofessional.

I was recommended to this clinic from a friend but I have been disappointed from the beginning. I initially contact them for fertility testing with a view to egg freezing and was excited that they could fit me in, were less expensive than other clinics, and were local to me. Unfortunately after I booked the appointment I received no confirmation email and was concerned the booking hadn't been made. I waited until after a long weekend to call them and when I spoke to someone she told me I'd been listed as an NHS patient instead of a private one and as such hadn't received the confirmation email. I asked if I could still have the appointment and she just kept saying "you have to pay" over and over again, as if I had deliberately tried not to pay. I explained that no one had ever billed me and I'd received no invoice. She confirmed I could keep the appointment and I then asked if she could move my follow up consultation earlier as they were making me wait for a week. She became strange at this point and said she had to check things with a manager and that someone would call me back.

6 hours later I received a phone call from them offering me the same consulation appointment. The woman also threatened that they would cancel the appointment if I didn't pay, again insinuating that I had deliberately tried not to pay. I told her I had called four times trying to confirm the appointment and at no point had I ever refused to pay. I then asked her to send me a confirmation email and paid almost £400 for the tests. She didn't send the confirmation email until the next day.

The scans themselves were fine and I had perfectly pleasant experience with the doctors and nurses there, but every time I tried to liaise with the admin it was awful. A week later I was supposed to have my follow up consultation and organised my work schedule around it. The doctor was then 15 minutes late calling me with no communication from anyone. I was just waiting by the phone at work. When the doctor finally called he didn't apologise for being late at all and gave me one of the most bland, yet functional consultations ever.

Of course I have been in search of a clinic to carry out the egg freezing process with and have had several consulations. I would have honestly loved to use Kings but it was impossible to trust them with the logistics of organising my procedure when my initial experience was so ridiculous. I'm sure the medical side is probably fine but I don't want to spend thousands to be mucked around by a clinic like this when there are so many others out there that treat you with a lot more respect for your time. Honestly really disappointed and didn't think this would be the case after my friend recommended them.

We're sorry to see that you have scored us a 1. We're unable to identify you, but we'd like to see if there's anything we can do. Please reach out to the team via info@kingsfertility.co.uk and one of our team will be able to help you.

I feel completely let down by this clinic. Nurses were always great and work hard, but the admin and treatment post failed transfer has been so sub optimal. I needed my notes for NHS records and still waiting 26 days later. Told policy is 28 days when time is of essence and when you need it most zero support it’s so disheartening.

Some of the consultants in this clinic lack proper bedside manners. Going through fertility treatment is hard enough - a bit more kindness and consideration would be appreciated. Also, no one takes the time to explain anything to you properly, so be prepared to inform yourself and ask a lot of questions.

Have being having horrible experience with Kings fertility , in term of appointment and miss information. The receptionist are too rigid. The services their is so frustrating. You can't get to talk to people in authority to listen to you. No doctor or nurses to do fellowup. I only get information from the receptionist.who is only insterested in your payment or you can't be book for your next appointment this is really frustrating and embracing on a reputable  hospital. Am really sad. I think am not one of the luckiness with the clinic. The experience have be so frustrating right from the beginning. Have being on this journey since last year, its almost end of the year, yet still on it. Asking to pay for an appointment I didn't cancel

Disappointed in how bookings for initial consultation are communicated. I have been told different information about the number and format of bookings required, I was told a virtual consultation is available after the testings are done and then it's not. Waste of time.

My experience with King’s Fertility was a mixed bag, and a few things stood out:

- illogical administrative processes, like the team starting a new email thread instead of replying directly to a query I’d sent. Which made it difficult to keep track of everything and find specific information.

- they do not provide consultation letters after each appointment. Which means you have no formal copy of what was discussed or the plan, unless you make your own notes.

- my consultant was friendly but clearly didn’t have much faith or creativity in managing my case if I didn’t jump straight into IVF. I have low amh but everything is still functioning well.

- I met a new clinician at every monitoring appointment and had to keep updating them. Some didn’t speak to me at all and others were fantastic.

- nurses were very warm and supportive.

- I requested my medical notes after our failed cycle and said I needed them urgently to take to an NHS appointment. It took longer than the 28 days they suggested to me and I ended up having to chase.

The clinic and the nursing team are excellent but I recommend avoiding miss Leila Hanna as a consultant. She has failed to listen to me as her patient and is not interested in my feelings and experiences and she wasn’t not great at communication with me during my IVF cycle. I’m sorry to give 3 to the clinic but I also want to give a word of advice to other patients

We are currently going through fertility treatment with Kings. Firstly I would highlight there isn’t anything wrong with the clinical side of things. However, the customer facing / communication side is very poor. There is one particular …

Some (but not all) of the admin staff on the phone can be condescending, frustrating, misinformed and insensitive - this made a difficult and emotional process more stressful. The clinical staff were outstanding and very supportive though. You don’t get to see the same doctor each time, but all were great.
